title: Leptinella filiformis
taxonomy: Leptinella filiformis belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It is a genus of plants that is native to New Zealand and parts of South America. This plant is classified as a flowering plant, with its classification being further broken down into the kingdom Plantae, and then the class Magnoliopsida. Within the class Magnoliopsida, it falls under the order Asterales. This classification helps us understand the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Leptinella filiformis.

intro: Leptinella filiformis, also known as Wire Plant or Dune Daisy, is a small, delicate flowering plant native to New Zealand, characterized by its thin, wiry stems and yellow or white flowers. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in reducing inflammation and preventing infections.
